name: Sierra de Camarena – circular from Teruel via Villaspesa
name_original: Sierra de Camarena – circular desde Teruel por Villaspesa
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Hoya De Teruel traverse a landscape characterized by diverse terrain, from the gentle gradients of converted railway lines to the more challenging ascents of mountainous regions. The area features striking natural formations like the reddish sandstone "Clays of Teruel" and the confluence of the Alfambra and Guadalaviar rivers. Cyclists can explore historical sites, charming villages, and remote natural settings, offering a blend of cultural immersion and outdoor adventure. This region provides a variety of paths suitable for different experience levels, from relaxed rides to multi-day expeditions. Best touring cycling routes around Hoya De Teruel The most popular touring cycling route is Old trains - circular from Teruel via the old railway network, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows parts of the historic railway network, offering a scenic journey. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ojos Negros Greenway and Camino del Cid Loop, a difficult 43.9 miles (70.8 km) path. This route combines sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros with the historic Camino del Cid, showcasing varied landscapes and cultural heritage. Local touring cyclists also love the Sierra de Camarena – circular from Teruel via Villaspesa, a 20.8 miles (33.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Camarena, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Hoya De Teruel is defined by Vías Verdes, the remote "Montañas Vacías," and the unique "Clays of Teruel" landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to challenging mountain routes. The routes in Hoya De Teruel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hoya De Teruel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Hoya de Teruel offers a wide selection of over 160 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging multi-day adventures, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Yes, Hoya de Teruel has several easy and moderate touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. The region is known for its Vías Verdes (Greenways), which are disused railway lines converted into cycling paths, offering gentle gradients and traffic-free experiences. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955806404/via-verde-de-ojos-negros-circular-from-mora-de-rubielos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Ojos Negros – circular from Mora de Rubielos&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 30.9 km path.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Hoya de Teruel offers numerous difficult routes with significant elevation changes. The region is part of the broader 'Montañas Vacías' (Empty Mountains) area, known for its remote and wild nature. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955647274/ojos-negros-greenway-and-camino-del-cid-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway and Camino del Cid Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 70.8 km route combining greenway sections with the historic Camino del Cid, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956599550/empty-mountains-circular-from-the-city-of-teruel-return-via-cella-canal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains – circular from the city of Teruel return via Cella Canal&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 73.9 km journey.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Hoya de Teruel region?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955857641/old-trains-circular-from-teruel-via-the-old-railway-network" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old trains - circular from Teruel via the old railway network&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.9 km moderate route. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955627244/sierra-de-camarena-circular-from-teruel-via-villaspesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Camarena – circular from Teruel via Villaspesa&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.5 km difficult trail.

Q: What kind of landscapes and attractions can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Hoya de Teruel's touring cycling routes offer a rich tapestry of landscapes and historical sites. You can cycle through the stunning reddish sandstone formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;, often called the 'Aragonese Colorado Canyon.' Routes also pass by the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and through charming villages. In Teruel city, you can admire Mudejar architecture at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1263073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teruel Cathedral Square&lt;/a&gt; and visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6140646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro and Mausoleum of the Lovers of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Hoya de Teruel?
A: The touring cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, from the accessible Vías Verdes to the challenging mountain routes, and the opportunity to combine natural beauty with cultural immersion in this unique region.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Hoya de Teruel?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Hoya de Teruel, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. The diverse terrain means that some routes may be accessible year-round, depending on weather conditions.

Q: Are there options for winter cycling in Hoya de Teruel?
A: While some higher elevation routes might be affected by snow or colder temperatures, many of the lower-lying Vías Verdes and routes around the city of Teruel can still be enjoyed during winter, especially on clear, sunny days. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts and route conditions before heading out during the colder months.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel pass through or near charming villages and towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, for instance, offers opportunities to stop in various settlements along its path. Planning your route in advance can help identify suitable refreshment stops.

Q: How can I access the touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: The city of Teruel serves as a central hub for the region and is accessible by public transport, including train and bus services. From Teruel, you can often connect to the starting points of various cycling routes. For routes further afield, local bus services might provide access to some villages, but it's recommended to check specific schedules and bicycle transport policies with local operators.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as common starting points for touring cycling routes. In Teruel city, there are various parking facilities. For routes like those along the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, you can often find parking in the nearby villages. Always look for designated parking areas to ensure convenience and safety.

Q: Are there any multi-day touring cycling adventures available in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Yes, for those seeking multi-day adventures, Hoya de Teruel is part of the 'Montañas Vacías' (Empty Mountains) bikepacking route, known for its remote and challenging nature. While not explicitly listed as a multi-day tour in the guide data, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956714546/mora-de-rubielos-rubielos-de-mora-circular-by-the-mijares-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mora de Rubielos - Rubielos de Mora – circular by the Mijares river&lt;/a&gt; (52.4 km) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956347125/empty-mountains-circular-through-the-puebla-de-san-miguel-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains - circular through the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; (40.3 km) can be integrated into longer expeditions, especially when combined with other sections of the 'Montañas Vacías' or the 'Three Taifas Trail' mentioned in regional research.

Q: What is the typical distance and elevation gain for touring cycling routes in the area?
A: The touring cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel vary significantly in distance and elevation. You can find shorter, flatter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955857641/old-trains-circular-from-teruel-via-the-old-railway-network" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old trains - circular from Teruel via the old railway network&lt;/a&gt;, which is 30.9 km with 433 meters of elevation gain. For more challenging rides, routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Plain – View of the "Wild West" loop from Puebla de Valverde&lt;/a&gt; cover 92.3 km with over 800 meters of elevation gain, offering a diverse range for all fitness levels.
tips: From this hermitage you have a good panoramic view of the town of Aldehuela and you can get to see Teruel.

This modernist building was built between 1911 and 1912 by the Catalan architect Pablo Monguió, who worked extensively in the community of Teruel at the beginning of the 20th century. It is an interpretation of medieval religious architecture, fusing historicist references with the aesthetic language of the early 20th century. It is located a short distance from the city of Teruel, about five kilometers.

Teruel's Cathedral Square is an iconic location in the heart of the city. It is home to the Cathedral of Santa María de Mediavilla, a jewel of Mudejar art that has been declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Built between the 12th and 16th centuries, the cathedral is notable for its tower, roof and dome, all of which are magnificent examples of the Mudejar style.

The square itself has an elongated shape and is a popular meeting point, especially during events such as the Isabel Wedding festivities, where it is filled with medieval stalls and activities.
